text:
REGIME — Risk bifurcated; semis and metals led, software and healthcare crushed.

INDICES — VIX 21.2, rising, VIXY +1.19%. Ranked: QQQ +0.14%, SPY -0.07%, IWM -0.25%, DIA -0.55%. Large caps outperformed small caps. WTI oil (USO) was the sharpest cross-asset mover, falling -1.69%.

SECTORS — XLB +0.56%, XLK +0.39%, XLRE +0.21%, XLY +0.13%, XLC -0.28%, XLI -0.39%, XLU -0.40%, XLE -0.68%, XLF -1.09%, XLP -1.29%, XLV -1.35%. Top industries: WGMI +2.01%, CPER +1.87%. Bottom industries: CIBR -3.71%, WCLD -5.01%.

THE 7s — Semi +2.74%, Buildout +1.37%, New Metals +1.35%, Old Metals +1.31%, Fab +1.02%, Power +0.03%, Housing -0.13%, Freight -0.14%, New Staples -0.18%, Bank -0.24%, Industrial -0.40%, Energy -0.57%, Medtech -0.84%, Old Staples -0.95%, Toll Road -1.39%, Health -1.44%, Defense -1.64%, Insurance -2.09%, Cloud -2.11%, Software -3.59%. SMH +1.53% anchored Semi leadership; NET -13.50% was the weakest ticker in Software.

FED & LIQUIDITY — Next FOMC Apr 29; CME holds 100% probability of no change at 3.50–3.75%. Fed composition: 6 hawks, 5 neutral, 4 doves. USD Liquidity Stress composite 56/100 (Elevated); most-stressed components are SOFR Dispersion and Reserve Scarcity, both at the 80th percentile. TGA fell -$99.3B, ON-RRP sits at $0.5B — Treasury drawdown adding reserves near-term.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed 59.6, Neutral. Top movers: NET -13.50% (Software), NOW -7.58% (Cloud), INTU -2.97% (Software). 20d momentum best sector: XLK +3.60%; worst: XLP -1.68%. Best basket 20d: Semi +18.98%; worst: Software -17.03%. NET's -13.50% collapse on 3.5x volume suggests an earnings or guidance miss driving the Software 7's -3.59% session low.

WATCH — NET: -13.50% today, 3.5x vol spike; watch for capitulation stabilization or continued institutional exit. NOW: -7.58%, 3.6x vol; gap risk into earnings season warrants level monitoring. PLTR: -1.86%, 2.7x vol spike despite Semi strength; divergence from its correlated basket worth flagging.
